pr.title: PR #146 â€” Execution Chain Orchestrator v1
pr.description:
## Summary M4 vertical slice: proves one deterministic capability (`self-model-build`) moves through the **existing** execution stack as a real producer/consumer chain with **runtime ID flow**, not adjacent framework objects nominally wired by docstrings. A thin `ExecutionChainOrchestrator` calls the existing engines in order and threads each stage's **real upstream id** forward as a typed reference. It introduces **no new execution object family** â€” `ExecutionChainTrace` is a view-only inspection container, not an engine-produced object. ``` self-model-build â†’ ExecutionPlanEngine.create() plan_id â†’ ExecutionStepEngine.create() step.ref(PLAN) == plan_id â†’ ExecutionAttemptEngine.create() attempt.ref(STEP) == step_id â†’ ExecutionResultEngine.create() result.ref(ATTEMPT)== attempt_id â†’ ExecutionArtifactEngine.create() artifact.ref(RESULT)== result_id â†’ evidence.json refs {result_id, artifact_id, capability_id} â†’ ExecutionReportEngine.create() report.ref(RESULT)==result_id, ref(ARTIFACT)==artifact_id, ref(FILE)==evidence_id ``` Every stage's record is **persisted to disk** by its engine (`report.json` + evidence per stage). `resolve_persisted(trace)` re-reads those files and walks `report â†’ artifact/result â†’ attempt â†’ step â†’ plan`, so the proof is against real persisted records â€” **not fabricated only inside the orchestrator response**. ### Selected capability and why `self-model-build` / `code-inventory` (M1 family): CI-safe, deterministic, no Revit runtime, already emits structured output + evidence. `SetParameterValue` deferred (hard Revit-runtime dep, non-deterministic); `InventoryModel` rejected (broad + guarded) â€” per the merged M4 packet. ### Existing engines reused `execution_plan`, `execution_step`, `execution_attempt_v2`, `execution_result`, `execution_artifact`, `execution_report`, `self_model` (code-inventory path), existing evidence/pass_fail behavior, existing CLI conventions. Only a thin orchestration adapter was added. ### Execution chain stages implemented Plan â†’ Step â†’ Attempt â†’ Result â†’ Artifact â†’ Evidence â†’ Report (all 7). ### ID-flow proof summary - 7 transitions each assert `downstream.reference_value == upstream.id` (non-placeholder; ids distinct). - End-to-end in-memory trace resolves `report â†’ artifact â†’ result â†’ attempt â†’ step â†’ plan`. - From-disk resolution resolves the same chain from each engine's persisted `report.json`. ### Static vs runtime relationship (honest accounting) - **Static import relationship:** `declared_but_unwired_chains` stays **5** on the real self-model. The gap analyzer only credits **direct adjacent `execution_* â†’ execution_*` import edges**; I deliberately did **not** add those just to score 5â†’0. The orchestrator does add **8 real edges** into the execution stack (`orchestrator â†’ each engine`, `cli â†’ orchestrator`). - **Runtime executable relationship:** **PASS** â€” orchestrator-mediated real ID flow with persisted/resolvable records, which is the accepted M4 proof. ### Tests and validation numbers - `tests/test_execution_chain_orchestrator.py`: **17 passed** (full chain + PASS status; id-equality per transition; per-edge transition record; evidence refs; report refs; in-memory e2e trace; **from-disk persisted resolution**; missing-upstream empty-container & missing-id; unsupported capability; broken id-flow rejected; determinism; `code-inventory` alias; M2/M3 hooks; gap metric responds to real wiring; CLI json + console). - `tests/test_command_registry.py`: **27 passed** (added `execution-chain-run` to catalog + expected set). - Full `poetry run pytest -q`: **5027 passed, 1 skipped** (the 1 prior failure was the command-registry catalog assertion for the new command, now fixed). - `ruff check` on changed files: clean. - CLI smoke: `axiom execution-chain-run --capability self-model-build --json-output` â†’ status **PASS**, all 7 ids emitted, all 7 transitions `ok=true`. ### CLI walkthrough summary `axiom execution-chain-run [--capability ] [--repo-root

] [--artifacts-root

] [--json-output]`. Output includes plan/step/attempt/result/artifact/evidence/report ids + final PASS/FAIL and per-transition OK marks. Artifacts written under `{artifacts_root}/execution_chain/{run_id}/` (`self_model.json`, `evidence.json`, `trace.json`). ### M2 hook summary Records evidence produced (metrics + references) and the fields it *could* affect (`capability_state`, `readiness`, `confidence`, `promotion_status`) via existing `global_capability_registry` / `capability_confidence`. **Full Evidence-to-Promotion not implemented.** ### M3 hook summary Records purpose (`Build deterministic repository self-model`), layer (`self_model`), consumer (`code-inventory`), intended use required to execute the capability. **Full Purpose/Layer Index not implemented.** ### Non-goals confirmed No new execution framework/object family; no full Execution Graph Synthesizer; no Organizational State schema; no full Evidence-to-Promotion; no full Purpose/Layer Index; no M5 work; no Revit-runtime validation; no broad refactor; no canonical file migration; not docs-only. ### Unresolved questions - Whether the gap analyzer should additionally credit orchestrator-mediated runtime flow (not just direct adjacent import edges) when reporting `declared_but_unwired_chains` â€” routed as an observation to Program 2, not changed here. ### Baseline impact 2024 Revit baseline unaffected; no Revit live validation required (capability is CI-safe and deterministic). Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7 Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev
